title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand
summary: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.
imageAlt: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand

fullArticle:
An artist's impression of MONA Bangkok on the banks of the Chao Phraya River. (Supplied)

Tasmania's Museum of Old and New Art (Mona) has signed a memorandum of understanding to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand.

Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like the Hobart museum.

The project is not yet under construction.

Hobart's Museum of Old and New Art (Mona) has announced plans to establish a new art museum in Bangkok, Thailand.

Mona was opened in 2011 by David Walsh on the banks of the River Derwent and is known for its provocative artworks, including a wall of vulvas and a machine that is fed twice daily and mimics the human digestive process.
